revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Gifu Gokoku Shrine]][[fr:Gifu Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:岐阜護國神社]][[ko:기후 호국신사]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Gifu Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Gifu Gokoku Shrine
岐阜護國神社 | image = Gifugokokujinjya5775.JPG | caption = Gifu Gokoku Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|26|12.1|N|136|46|27.8|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = 1940 | date_destroyed = | location = 393 Mitarashi, {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|qid=Q45798|lt=Gifu|1=Gifu}}
{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} [[:en:〒|〒]]500-8002
Japan {{flagicon|Japan}} | website = {{URL|http://www.gifu-gokoku.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[File:岐阜護國神社-鳥居から本殿を望むGokokujinja001.jpg|thumb|Main gate to Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] [[File:岐阜護國神社-狛犬Gokokujinja004.jpg|thumb|[[:en:Imperial guardian lion|Lion of Buddha]]]] {{nihongo|'''Gifu Gokoku Shrine'''|岐阜護國神社|Gifu Gokoku Jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located at the base of {{ill|Mount Kinka (Gifu)|en|Mount Kinka (Gifu)|qid=Q6921671|lt=Mount Kinka|1=Mount Kinka (Gifu)}} in {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|qid=Q45798|lt=Gifu|1=Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The shrine is dedicated to the 37,000 residents of Gifu Prefecture who have died in wars since the [[Meiji Restoration]].[http://www.gifu-gokoku.com/index2.html History] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080625212702/http://www.gifu-gokoku.com/index2.html |date=2008-06-25 }}. {{in lang|ja}} Gifu Gokoku Shrine. Accessed June 19, 2008. Official ceremonies are also held at the neighboring Hotel Seiran, which is part of the shrine facilities. The shrine's two festivals are April 12 and October 5. ==History== During the first part of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, there were two shrines in Gifu Prefecture dedicated to the war dead. In [[:en:Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki]], there was the Ōgaki Shōkonsha (大垣招魂社), which was later renamed the [[Nōhi Gokoku Shrine]]. In {{ill|Takayama, Gifu|en|Takayama, Gifu|qid=Q622594|lt=Takayama|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, there was the Hida Shōkonsha (飛騨招魂社), which was later renamed the [[Hida Gokoku Shrine]]. These two shrines, however, only served their surrounding areas and there were many districts within the prefecture who did not have shrines dedicated to their war dead. After the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|qid=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War|1=Russo-Japanese War}} ended in 1905, in order to rectify the situation, plans for construction of a new shrine began to come together.. Construction would not begin, however, until 1939, when permission was finally granted to build the shrine. It was completed in 1940, and named Gifu Gokoku Shrine. After {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, the shrine was renamed '''Mino Mitama Shrine''' (美濃御霊神社 ''Mino Mitama Jinja''), but the shrine was later reverted to its original name. ==Cherry blossoms== Gifu Gokoku Shrine is well known for its cherry blossoms (''[[:en:Sakura|Sakura]]'') in the spring. Its ''ukai-zakura'' (鵜飼桜) is among the earliest bloomers in the city and was planted over 100 years ago.[http://www.gifucvb.or.jp/event/detail_spring.php?eid=00060&syu=2&season=spring&shiki_keisai=1&cmsdsessionid=167300120a531585d96a06cf20697078 Gifu's Four Seasons: Spring]. {{in lang|ja}} Gifu City Convention and Visitors Bureau. Accessed June 19, 2008. The tree trunk's circumference is about {{Convert|2.5|m|ft|1}} and the tree is about {{Convert|8|m|ft|1}} tall. The cherry trees received their names because it is said that their blossoms can indicate the amount of ''{{ill|Ayu sweetfish|en|Ayu sweetfish|qid=Q642906|lt=ayu|1=Ayu sweetfish}}'' caught during the upcoming [[:en:Cormorant Fishing on the Nagara River|cormorant fishing]] (''ukai'') season. ==See also== *[[Nōhi Gokoku Shrine]] *[[Hida Gokoku Shrine]] ==References== {{Reflist}} == images == {{Commons Category|Gifu-Gokoku-jinja}} File:岐阜護國神社-本殿Gokokujinja003.jpg File:岐阜護國神社-本殿屋根Gokokujinja008.jpg File:岐阜護國神社-拝殿Gokokujinja006.jpg File:岐阜護國神社-泰安殿Gokokujinja010.jpg File:岐阜護國神社-立石群Gokokujinja011.jpg File:岐阜護國神社-招魂祭場Gokokujinja016.jpg File:岐阜護國神社-鎮霊舎Gokokujinja018.jpg File:岐阜護國神社-ご神木の楠Gokokujinja020.jpg {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ [[en:Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] {{Commons category|Gifu-Gokoku-jinja}} [[fr:Gifu Gokoku-jinja]] [[ja:岐阜護國神社]] [[ko:기후 호국신사]] {{wikidata link|Q870911}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Gifu]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
